commercial_alignment_check: NOT_AVAILABLE — requires pre-negotiation commercial charter as input. See DECISIONS.md.

escalation_items.summary: StayLink granted unilateral right to amend commission rates quarterly — violates POL-001. Commercial cannot sign off until mutual agreement is restored.
escalation_items.decision_needed: Revert to mutual agreement requirement for commission amendments, or define acceptable bounds for StayLink-initiated reviews before proceeding.

escalation_items.summary: StayLink indemnification carve-out added unilaterally covering Nexus integration decisions — violates POL-001. Legal cannot sign off until renegotiated.
escalation_items.decision_needed: Remove unilateral carve-out or renegotiate to reflect mutual understanding of integration responsibilities.

critical_issues.description: Commission review shifted to unilateral StayLink control — quarterly, based on market conditions with 30-day notice only
StayLink indemnification obligations carved out for Nexus integration decisions, unilaterally added

compound_risks.description: Commission control loss (C1) + API suspension trigger (C2) + uncapped liability carve-out (C3) create a performance trap — a volume dip triggers reduced revenue, service disruption, and potentially unlimited financial liability simultaneously
compound_risks.decision_required: Model worst-case scenario: volume dip triggers all three simultaneously. Decide whether to accept any of these clauses before addressing the others — they are interdependent.

compound_risks.description: API suspension (C2) + uncapped liability (C3) mean a volume shortfall causes both operational shutdown and unlimited financial exposure to StayLink for the same trigger
compound_risks.decision_required: C2 and C3 must be negotiated together — accepting one without the other leaves compounded exposure. Do not allow Legal to sign off on C3 before Product/Tech has reviewed C2.
